headlines.text: Trump warns Iran to 'get smart soon' as peace talks stall and Strait of Hormuz stays closed
Hegseth grilled by Congress for first time since Iran war began, defends $1.5T defense budget
UAE announces it will quit OPEC effective May 1, rattling global energy markets
FCC challenges ABC station licenses after Trump demands action against Jimmy Kimmel
King Charles III addresses Congress in only second-ever speech by a British monarch, then heads to NYC
Brent crude surges past $116/barrel in eighth straight session of gains — IEA calls it largest supply shock on record
Fed expected to hold rates steady in what may be Jerome Powell's final meeting as chair
Mag 7 earnings — Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft — all report after the bell Wednesday
Tornado tears through Mineral Wells, Texas as severe storm outbreak sweeps Midwest and South
Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down Sept. 1; hardware chief John Ternus tapped as successor

breaking.text: Iran war Day 61: Trump posts AI-generated image of himself with rifle, warns Iran 'better get smart soon' as Pakistan awaits revised peace proposal by Friday

stories.hed: Trump Threatens Iran While Playing War-Themed Dress-Up on Social Media
stories.dek: With peace talks stalled and the Strait of Hormuz still closed, Trump posted an AI-generated image of himself holding a rifle with explosions behind him, warning Iran to 'get smart soon.' Pakistan is waiting on Iran's revised proposal by Friday — a deadline that sounds optimistic given Iran's rial just hit 1.8 million to the dollar.

stories.hed: UAE Ditches OPEC Just as Global Oil Supply Already Hanging by a Thread
stories.dek: The United Arab Emirates announced it will exit OPEC effective May 1, citing the Strait of Hormuz closure as the moment to break free and gain pricing flexibility. The IEA called the Hormuz shutdown the largest oil supply shock on record, and Brent crude hit $116 a barrel Wednesday.

stories.hed: FCC Targets ABC Licenses as Trump Escalates War on Jimmy Kimmel Over a Joke
stories.dek: The Trump administration challenged ABC's broadcast licenses — an extraordinary use of FCC power — after Trump demanded the network punish Kimmel for calling Melania an 'expectant widow.' Kimmel's Monday monologue hit 4 million views in under 24 hours, which is presumably not the chilling effect the White House was hoping for.

stories.hed: James Comey Indicted in Case Widely Seen as Targeting Protected Speech
stories.dek: The Trump administration secured an indictment against former FBI Director James Comey over conduct that legal experts say likely falls under First Amendment protection. It comes the same week the FCC went after ABC, prompting critics to describe a coordinated campaign to reshape what Americans see and hear in media.

stories.hed: New US Passports Will Feature Trump's Image Inside — Presumably So You Remember Who Signed Your Travel Docs
stories.dek: The State Department announced new passports will include an image of President Trump alongside Declaration of Independence text and the American flag, as part of the 250th anniversary branding. It is, analysts note, the most prominent use yet of a sitting president's likeness on official government documents Americans are legally required to carry.

markets.context: Markets drifted lower as oil surged on stalled Iran peace talks and a closed Strait of Hormuz, with all eyes on Mag 7 earnings after the bell and Powell's likely final Fed rate decision.

gas.note: Every time Iran and the US fail to negotiate, your commute gets 5 cents more expensive — it's basically a war tax on your morning drive.

popCulture.hed: Michael Jackson Biopic Moonwalks to Record $97M Domestic Opening, $219M Worldwide
popCulture.dek: Lionsgate's Michael, starring Jackson's nephew Jaafar Jackson and directed by Antoine Fuqua, shattered the all-time record for a music biopic opening — critics panned it as sanitized (38% on Rotten Tomatoes), but audiences didn't care and danced in the aisles anyway. It's already the biggest hit for Lionsgate since The Hunger Games: Mockingjay Part 2 in 2015.

genZ.definition: After a four-year absence, Euphoria returned in April 2026 with a five-year time jump and a new cast addition in Rosalía, igniting a TikTok wildfire of outfit recreations, quote-based audio trends, and 'glow-up' comparison edits every Sunday night. The pent-up demand turned every episode drop into a cultural event that basically shuts down fashion and beauty TikTok for 48 hours.
genZ.usage: Trump posting that AI rifle image is giving very much Nate Jacobs villain-era energy — chaotic, threatening, and somehow still on your feed.

tldr: Day 61 of the Iran war brought Trump posting AI action-hero selfies, Brent crude topping $116, the UAE rage-quitting OPEC, and gas hitting $4.23 a gallon — while Washington simultaneously tried to cancel late-night comedy and stamp the president's face inside your passport. The Michael Jackson biopic opened to $97M and people literally danced in theaters, which is either escapism or a cry for help.
